description: Fix broken sidebar layout (stuck at 5px) by removing it from react-resizable-panels and using CSS flex with custom drag handle. Plus UI polish: compact header, collapsible staging area, visual refinements.

userStories.title: Remove Sidebar from react-resizable-panels
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove the sidebar from react-resizable-panels so it renders at a usable width instead of being crushed to 5px.
acceptanceCriteria: The sidebar is NOT wrapped in a <Panel> component — it is a plain <div> child of .app-body
The sidebar <div> has style={{ width: sidebarWidth, flexShrink: 0, height: '100%', overflow: 'hidden' }} where sidebarWidth defaults to 260
Remove handleSidebarResize callback, sidebarPanelRef, and handleSidebarDividerDoubleClick from AppLayout (all related to the sidebar Panel)
Remove the <Separator> between sidebar and center panel
When sidebarCollapsed is true, render <Sidebar collapsed={true}> inside a 48px wide div (the icon rail)
When sidebarCollapsed is false, render <Sidebar collapsed={false}> inside the sidebarWidth div
Both left and right sidebar positions work (sidebar div rendered before or after the Group in flex container)
The center content (react-resizable-panels Group) takes remaining space via flex: 1 and min-width: 0
Border between sidebar and center: border-right: 1px solid var(--border) for left position, border-left for right
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: sidebar shows at 260px with branches/files content visible and scrollable
userStories.notes: This is the critical fix. The sidebar <Panel> inside react-resizable-panels is what causes the 5px crush bug. AppLayout.tsx is the primary file. The Sidebar.tsx component itself needs NO changes — it receives collapsed prop and renders accordingly. The .app-body class already uses display: flex.

userStories.title: Custom Drag Handle for Sidebar Resize
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag the sidebar edge to resize it so I can adjust how much space branches and files get.
acceptanceCriteria: A 5px wide drag handle <div> is rendered between the sidebar div and the center panel
The drag handle shows cursor: col-resize on hover
The drag handle shows a subtle background highlight on hover: background var(--border)
Dragging the handle resizes the sidebar width in real time via mousedown on handle, mousemove on document, mouseup on document
Minimum sidebar width enforced at 180px during drag
Maximum sidebar width enforced at 400px during drag
Double-clicking the handle resets sidebar to 260px default
Drag state tracked via useRef (not useState) to avoid re-renders during drag — only update state on mouseup or with requestAnimationFrame
Drag works correctly for right sidebar position (drag direction reversed)
The drag handle is only visible when sidebar is expanded (not when icon rail)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: drag handle appears, dragging resizes sidebar smoothly
userStories.notes: Depends on US-SB-001. The drag handle is a simple div with mouse event listeners. Use document-level mousemove/mouseup listeners so the drag continues even if cursor leaves the handle. Use useCallback for handlers. Consider adding a CSS class 'dragging' to body during drag to prevent text selection.

userStories.title: Persist Sidebar Width and Migrate Corrupt State
userStories.description: As a user, I want my sidebar width to persist across sessions, and any corrupt stored values from the old Panel-based sidebar must be fixed automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: sidebarSize in useLayoutState stores pixel width (integer, e.g. 260) not percentage
The DEFAULT_LAYOUT.sidebarSize is 260 (pixels)
On load in loadLayout(), if sidebarSize < 100 (was a percentage from old code), replace with 260
On load, if sidebarSize < 180 or > 400, clamp to 180-400 range
setSidebarSize(size) in useLayoutState updates sidebarSize in layout state
Width is saved with existing 300ms debounce (already in useLayoutState useEffect)
AppLayout reads layout.sidebarSize for the sidebar div width
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-SB-001. The existing useLayoutState already has setSidebarSize and debounced save. Just need to change the semantics from percentage to pixels and add the migration/clamping in loadLayout(). The onResize guard that rejects <12% values should be removed since we no longer use percentages.

userStories.title: Sidebar Toggle Without Crashes
userStories.description: As a user, I want Ctrl+B and the collapse button to toggle between expanded sidebar and icon rail reliably without any crashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+B cycles: expanded sidebar (260px+) → icon rail (48px) → expanded sidebar
The collapse button (PanelLeftClose/PanelLeftOpen icon) in sidebar header triggers same toggle
When expanded: sidebar div has width from layout.sidebarSize, full sidebar content visible and scrollable
When icon rail: sidebar div has width 48px, shows section icons vertically, clicking an icon opens floating overlay
CSS transition on the sidebar div: transition: width 200ms ease-out
The 'Invalid panel layout' error NEVER occurs (since sidebar is no longer a Panel)
Toggling rapidly 20 times produces no visual glitch, crash, or error
Collapse state persists across restarts via sidebarCollapsed in layout state
The responsive auto-collapse logic (< 900px → icon rail) continues to work
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: Ctrl+B toggles between expanded and icon rail smoothly
userStories.notes: Depends on US-SB-001. The toggleSidebar function in useLayoutState.ts cycles expanded→collapsed→expanded. Since the sidebar is now a plain div, toggling just changes sidebarCollapsed which switches between the 260px div and 48px icon rail div. No Panel mounting/unmounting. The responsive auto-collapse effect in AppLayout should continue to call setSidebarCollapsed.

userStories.title: Compact Repo Header — Remove Summary Cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want the repo header to be compact so the commit graph and staging area get more vertical space.
acceptanceCriteria: The 4 large summary cards (Current Branch, Staged, Unstaged, Untracked) are removed from RepoView
The repo name + path header is reduced to ~40px height (from ~60px)
The branch name is already shown in the titlebar center and status bar left — no need to duplicate it
Staged/unstaged/untracked counts are already shown in the Staging Area section headers — no need to duplicate
The Close button and refresh button remain in the compact header
Net vertical space saved is at least 80px compared to previous layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: compact header, commit graph has significantly more visible rows
userStories.notes: The summary cards are in RepoView.tsx. They show info that's already available in other parts of the UI (titlebar, status bar, staging area headers). Removing them frees ~80-100px of vertical space. Keep the repo name/path header but make it more compact.

userStories.title: Collapsible Staging Area
userStories.description: As a user, I want to collapse the staging area to a single header bar so the commit graph gets maximum vertical space when I'm not staging files.
acceptanceCriteria: The Staging Area header ('Staging Area · N files') is always visible as a clickable bar
Clicking the header or the existing chevron toggles the staging area open/closed
When closed: only the ~30px header bar visible, commit graph takes all remaining vertical space
When open: full staging area with file lists and commit form (current behavior)
The chevron icon rotates 90 degrees on collapse (already partially implemented)
Staging area collapse state persists in layout state (add stagingCollapsed boolean)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: collapsed staging gives commit graph much more rows
userStories.notes: The staging area already has a collapsible header with a chevron in StatusPanel.tsx. This story ensures the collapse actually hides the file lists and commit form, and that the state persists. May need to add stagingCollapsed to LayoutState in useLayoutState.ts.

userStories.title: Visual Polish — Transitions, Borders, Cleanup
userStories.description: As a user, I want the sidebar and layout to feel polished with smooth transitions and no visual artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar expand/collapse has smooth 200ms ease-out width transition (CSS transition property on the sidebar div)
Drag handle shows 3 subtle vertical dots (⋮) centered vertically on hover, using var(--text-muted) color
All panel borders use consistent var(--border) color with no orphaned gaps
The 'Filter History' bar (CommitFilterBar) renders correctly within the center panel — not clipped behind the sidebar
No text peeking out at 2px on the left edge (the old 5px sidebar ghost is completely gone)
No layout flash or jitter when toggling sidebar or opening a repo
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: polished transitions, no visual artifacts, filter bar fully visible
userStories.notes: Final polish pass. The Filter History bar was previously clipped at the left edge — verify it renders correctly inside the center panel now that the sidebar is outside react-resizable-panels. Check for any residual CSS that referenced the old sidebar Panel.
